    26ŌåÆ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    27ŌåÆ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    28ŌåÆ)
    29ŌåÆ
    30ŌåÆ// OrchestratorStore defines all data access methods required by the agent
    31ŌåÆ// orchestrator to load project configuration, run the agent, and persist
    32ŌåÆ// the resulting semantic release.
    33ŌåÆtype OrchestratorStore interface {
    34ŌåÆ	AgentDataStore
    35ŌåÆ	GetProject(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Project, error)
    36ŌåÆ	GetAgentRun(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.AgentRun, error)
    37ŌåÆ	UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx context.Context, id, status string) error
    38ŌåÆ	CreateSemanticRelease(ctx context.Context, sr *models.SemanticRelease, releaseIDs []string) error
    39ŌåÆ	UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx context.Context, id string, semanticReleaseID string) error
    40ŌåÆ	ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx context.Context, projectID string) ([]models.Subscription, error)
    41ŌåÆ	GetChannel(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.NotificationChannel, error)
    42ŌåÆ	ListSourcesByProject(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int) ([]models.Source, int, error)
    43ŌåÆ	HasReleaseForVersion(ctx context.Context, sourceID, version string) (bool, error)
    44ŌåÆ}
    45ŌåÆ
    46ŌåÆconst DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.
    47ŌåÆ
    48ŌåÆFocus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.
    49ŌåÆ
    50ŌåÆUse the available tools to:
    51ŌåÆ1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
    52ŌåÆ2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
    53ŌåÆ3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
    54ŌåÆ4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
    55ŌåÆ5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
    56ŌåÆ   (e.g., community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues).
    57ŌåÆ
    58ŌåÆCRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
    59ŌåÆDo not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting ŌĆö just the raw JSON.
    60ŌåÆ
    61ŌåÆFor download_links: prefer direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) over generic release pages. Include the general release page URL as well, but prioritize direct download links when available.
    62ŌåÆ
    63ŌåÆThe JSON object must have exactly these fields:
    64ŌåÆ{
    65ŌåÆ  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
    66ŌåÆ  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
    67ŌåÆ  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
    68ŌåÆ  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
    69ŌåÆ  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
    70ŌåÆ  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
    71ŌåÆ  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
    72ŌåÆ  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation for the SRE team",
    73ŌåÆ  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15"],
    74ŌåÆ  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
    75ŌåÆ}`
    76ŌåÆ
    77ŌåÆ// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    78ŌåÆ// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    79ŌåÆ// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    80ŌåÆfunc BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    81ŌåÆ	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    82ŌåÆ	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    83ŌåÆ		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    84ŌåÆ	}
    85ŌåÆ	instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    86ŌåÆ
    87ŌåÆ	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, nil)
    88ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
    89ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    90ŌåÆ	}
    91ŌåÆ
    92ŌåÆ	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    93ŌåÆ	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    94ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
    95ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
    96ŌåÆ	}
    97ŌåÆ
    98ŌåÆ	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
    99ŌåÆ	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   100ŌåÆ		Name:        "data_agent",
   101ŌåÆ		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   102ŌåÆ		Model:       llmModel,
   103ŌåÆ		Tools:       functionTools,
   104ŌåÆ	})
   105ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   106ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   107ŌåÆ	}
   108ŌåÆ
   109ŌåÆ	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   110ŌåÆ	var searchTool tool.Tool
   111ŌåÆ	var searchModel model.LLM
   112ŌåÆ	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   113ŌåÆ	case "openai":
   114ŌåÆ		wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
   115ŌåÆ		searchTool = *wsConfig
   116ŌåÆ		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
   117ŌåÆ		if err != nil {
   118ŌåÆ			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   119ŌåÆ		}
   120ŌåÆ	default: // gemini
   121ŌåÆ		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   122ŌåÆ		searchModel = llmModel
   123ŌåÆ	}
   124ŌåÆ
   125ŌåÆ	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   126ŌåÆ		Name:        "search_agent",
   127ŌåÆ		Description: "Search the web for additional context about a release. Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources, such as community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption statistics, or known issues.",
   128ŌåÆ		Model:       searchModel,
   129ŌåÆ		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   130ŌåÆ	})
   131ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   132ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   133ŌåÆ	}
   134ŌåÆ
   135ŌåÆ	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   136ŌåÆ	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   137ŌåÆ		Name:        "release_analyst",
   138ŌåÆ		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   139ŌåÆ		Model:       llmModel,
   140ŌåÆ		Instruction: instruction,
   141ŌåÆ		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   142ŌåÆ			agenttool.New(dataAgent, nil),
   143ŌåÆ			agenttool.New(searchAgent, nil),
   144ŌåÆ		},
   145ŌåÆ	})
   146ŌåÆ}
   147ŌåÆ
   148ŌåÆ// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   149ŌåÆ// release matching the target version.
   150ŌåÆfunc (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   151ŌåÆ	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   152ŌåÆ	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   153ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   154ŌåÆ		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   155ŌåÆ	}
   156ŌåÆ	if len(sources) == 0 {
   157ŌåÆ		return true, nil
   158ŌåÆ	}
   159ŌåÆ
   160ŌåÆ	for _, src := range sources {
   161ŌåÆ		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   162ŌåÆ		if err != nil {
   163ŌåÆ			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   164ŌåÆ		}
   165ŌåÆ		if !has {
   166ŌåÆ			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",
   167ŌåÆ				"project_id", projectID,
   168ŌåÆ				"source_id", src.ID,
   169ŌåÆ				"version", version,
   170ŌåÆ			)
   171ŌåÆ			return false, nil
   172ŌåÆ		}
   173ŌåÆ	}
   174ŌåÆ	return true, nil
   175ŌåÆ}
   176ŌåÆ
   177ŌåÆ// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   178ŌåÆ// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   179ŌåÆ// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   180ŌåÆtype Orchestrator struct {
   181ŌåÆ	store     OrchestratorStore
   182ŌåÆ	llmConfig LLMConfig
   183ŌåÆ	publicURL string // base URL for internal Changelogue links (e.g. "https://changelogue.example.com")
   184ŌåÆ}
   185ŌåÆ
   186ŌåÆ// NewOrchestrator creates a new Orchestrator. It validates that the provided
   187ŌåÆ// LLMConfig has the required API key for the configured provider. If the key
   188ŌåÆ// is missing, it returns an error so the caller can degrade gracefully.
   189ŌåÆfunc NewOrchestrator(store OrchestratorStore, cfg LLMConfig, publicURL string) (*Orchestrator, error) {
   190ŌåÆ	switch cfg.Provider {
   191ŌåÆ	case "openai":
   192ŌåÆ		if cfg.OpenAIAPIKey == "" {
   193ŌåÆ			return nil, fmt.Errorf("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   194ŌåÆ		}
   195ŌåÆ	default: // gemini
   196ŌåÆ		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
   197ŌåÆ			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   198ŌåÆ		}
   199ŌåÆ	}
   200ŌåÆ	return &Orchestrator{
   201ŌåÆ		store:     store,
   202ŌåÆ		llmConfig: cfg,
   203ŌåÆ		publicURL: strings.TrimRight(publicURL, "/"),
   204ŌåÆ	}, nil
   205ŌåÆ}
   206ŌåÆ
   207ŌåÆ// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   208ŌåÆ//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   209ŌåÆ//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   210ŌåÆ//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   211ŌåÆ//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   212ŌåÆ//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   213ŌåÆ//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   214ŌåÆ//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   215ŌåÆfunc (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   216ŌåÆ	// Mark as running.
   217ŌåÆ	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   218ŌåÆ		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   219ŌåÆ	}
   220ŌåÆ
   221ŌåÆ	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   222ŌåÆ	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   223ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   224ŌåÆ		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   225ŌåÆ		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   226ŌåÆ		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   227ŌåÆ			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   228ŌåÆ				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   229ŌåÆ		}
   230ŌåÆ		return err
   231ŌåÆ	}
   232ŌåÆ
   233ŌåÆ	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   234ŌåÆ	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   235ŌåÆ		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   236ŌåÆ	}
   237ŌåÆ	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   238ŌåÆ		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   239ŌåÆ	}
   240ŌåÆ
   241ŌåÆ	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   242ŌåÆ	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   243ŌåÆ
   244ŌåÆ	return nil
   245ŌåÆ}
   246ŌåÆ
   247ŌåÆ// agentResult holds the output of a successful agent execution, used to
   248ŌåÆ// construct notifications after the semantic release is persisted.
   249ŌåÆtype agentResult struct {
   250ŌåÆ	semanticReleaseID string
   251ŌåÆ	version           string
   252ŌåÆ	reportText        string
   253ŌåÆ	projectName       string
   254ŌåÆ}
   255ŌåÆ
   256ŌåÆ// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   257ŌåÆ// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   258ŌåÆfunc (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   259ŌåÆ	// Load project.
   260ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   261ŌåÆ	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   262ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   263ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   264ŌåÆ	}
   265ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   266ŌåÆ
   267ŌåÆ	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   268ŌåÆ	version := run.Version
   269ŌåÆ	if version == "" {
   270ŌåÆ		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   271ŌåÆ		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   272ŌåÆ			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   273ŌåÆ		}
   274ŌåÆ	}
   275ŌåÆ
   276ŌåÆ	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   277ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   278ŌåÆ		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   279ŌåÆ		"version", version)
   280ŌåÆ	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   281ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   282ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("build agent: %w", err)
   283ŌåÆ	}
   284ŌåÆ
   285ŌåÆ	// Create in-memory session service and a new session.
   286ŌåÆ	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   287ŌåÆ	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   288ŌåÆ		AppName: "changelogue",
   289ŌåÆ		UserID:  "system",
   290ŌåÆ	})
   291ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   292ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   293ŌåÆ	}
   294ŌåÆ	sess := createResp.Session
   295ŌåÆ
   296ŌåÆ	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   297ŌåÆ	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   298ŌåÆ		AppName:        "changelogue",
   299ŌåÆ		Agent:          agentInstance,
   300ŌåÆ		SessionService: sessionService,
   301ŌåÆ		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   302ŌåÆ			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   303ŌåÆ			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   304ŌåÆ		},
   305ŌåÆ	})
   306ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   307ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   308ŌåÆ	}
   309ŌåÆ
   310ŌåÆ	// Run the agent with a prompt requesting analysis.
   311ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: starting LLM run", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name, "version", version)
   312ŌåÆ	var userPrompt string
   313ŌåÆ	if version != "" {
   314ŌåÆ		userPrompt = fmt.Sprintf("Analyze version %s for this project. Cross-check all sources and produce a semantic release report.", version)
   315ŌåÆ	} else {
   316ŌåÆ		userPrompt = "Analyze the recent releases for this project and produce a semantic release report."
   317ŌåÆ	}
   318ŌåÆ	userMsg := genai.NewContentFromText(userPrompt, "user")
   319ŌåÆ
   320ŌåÆ	var finalText string
   321ŌåÆ	eventCount := 0
   322ŌåÆ	for event, err := range r.Run(ctx, "system", sess.ID(), userMsg, agent.RunConfig{}) {
   323ŌåÆ		if err != nil {
   324ŌåÆ			slog.Error("agent: run event error",
   325ŌåÆ				"run_id", run.ID,
   326ŌåÆ				"event_count", eventCount,
   327ŌåÆ				"err", err,
   328ŌåÆ			)
   329ŌåÆ			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent run event error: %w", err)
   330ŌåÆ		}
   331ŌåÆ		eventCount++
   332ŌåÆ		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   333ŌåÆ			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   334ŌåÆ				if part.Text != "" {
   335ŌåÆ					finalText += part.Text
   336ŌåÆ				}
   337ŌåÆ			}
   338ŌåÆ		}
   339ŌåÆ	}
   340ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",
   341ŌåÆ		"run_id", run.ID,
   342ŌåÆ		"event_count", eventCount,
   343ŌåÆ		"output_length", len(finalText),
   344ŌåÆ	)
   345ŌåÆ
   346ŌåÆ	if finalText == "" {
   347ŌåÆ		slog.Error("agent: produced no output",
   348ŌåÆ			"run_id", run.ID,
   349ŌåÆ			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   350ŌåÆ			"event_count", eventCount,
   351ŌåÆ		)
   352ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent produced no output")
   353ŌåÆ	}
   354ŌåÆ
   355ŌåÆ	// Parse the agent's response into a SemanticReport.
   356ŌåÆ	report, err := parseReport(finalText)
   357ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   358ŌåÆ		slog.Warn("agent output was not valid JSON report, storing raw",
   359ŌåÆ			"run_id", run.ID, "parse_err", err, "raw_output", finalText)
   360ŌåÆ		// Fall back to storing the raw text as the summary.
   361ŌåÆ		report = &models.SemanticReport{
   362ŌåÆ			Summary: finalText,
   363ŌåÆ		}
   364ŌåÆ	}
   365ŌåÆ
   366ŌåÆ	reportJSON, err := json.Marshal(report)
   367ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   368ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal report: %w", err)
   369ŌåÆ	}
   370ŌåÆ
   371ŌåÆ	// Gather release IDs for the semantic_release_sources join table.
   372ŌåÆ	// Only link releases that match the target version.
   373ŌåÆ	releases, _, err := o.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 200, false)
   374ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   375ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list releases for semantic release: %w", err)
   376ŌåÆ	}
   377ŌåÆ	releaseIDs := make([]string, 0, len(releases))
   378ŌåÆ	for _, r := range releases {
   379ŌåÆ		if r.Version == version {
   380ŌåÆ			releaseIDs = append(releaseIDs, r.ID)
   381ŌåÆ		}
   382ŌåÆ	}
   383ŌåÆ
   384ŌåÆ	// Use the target version for the semantic release.
   385ŌåÆ	srVersion := version
   386ŌåÆ	if srVersion == "" {
   387ŌåÆ		srVersion = "unknown"
   388ŌåÆ		if len(releases) > 0 {
   389ŌåÆ			srVersion = releases[0].Version
   390ŌåÆ		}
   391ŌåÆ	}
   392ŌåÆ
   393ŌåÆ	now := time.Now()
   394ŌåÆ	sr := &models.SemanticRelease{
   395ŌåÆ		ProjectID:   run.ProjectID,
   396ŌåÆ		Version:     srVersion,
   397ŌåÆ		Report:      reportJSON,
   398ŌåÆ		Status:      "completed",
   399ŌåÆ		CompletedAt: &now,
   400ŌåÆ	}
   401ŌåÆ
   402ŌåÆ	if err := o.store.CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs); err != nil {
   403ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create semantic release: %w", err)
   404ŌåÆ	}
   405ŌåÆ
   406ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent run produced semantic release",
   407ŌåÆ		"run_id", run.ID,
   408ŌåÆ		"semantic_release_id", sr.ID,
   409ŌåÆ		"version", sr.Version,
   410ŌåÆ	)
   411ŌåÆ
   412ŌåÆ	return &agentResult{
   413ŌåÆ		semanticReleaseID: sr.ID,
   414ŌåÆ		version:           srVersion,
   415ŌåÆ		reportText:        finalText,
   416ŌåÆ		projectName:       project.Name,
   417ŌåÆ	}, nil
   418ŌåÆ}
   419ŌåÆ
   420ŌåÆ// defaultSenders returns a map of channel type to Sender for the supported
   421ŌåÆ// notification channel types (webhook, slack, discord).
   422ŌåÆfunc defaultSenders() map[string]routing.Sender {
   423ŌåÆ	httpClient := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   424ŌåÆ	return map[string]routing.Sender{
   425ŌåÆ		"webhook": &routing.WebhookSender{Client: httpClient},
   426ŌåÆ		"slack":   &routing.SlackSender{Client: httpClient},
   427ŌåÆ		"discord": &routing.DiscordSender{Client: httpClient},
   428ŌåÆ	}
   429ŌåÆ}
   430ŌåÆ
   431ŌåÆ// sendProjectNotifications looks up project-level subscriptions and sends a
   432ŌåÆ// notification to each channel with the semantic release report. This is
   433ŌåÆ// best-effort: errors are logged but do not propagate to the caller.
   434ŌåÆfunc (o *Orchestrator) sendProjectNotifications(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun, result *agentResult) {
   435ŌåÆ	subs, err := o.store.ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   436ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   437ŌåÆ		slog.Error("list project subscriptions for notification",
   438ŌåÆ			"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
   439ŌåÆ		return
   440ŌåÆ	}
   441ŌåÆ	if len(subs) == 0 {
   442ŌåÆ		slog.Debug("agent: no project subscriptions configured",
   443ŌåÆ			"project_id", run.ProjectID)
   444ŌåÆ		return
   445ŌåÆ	}
   446ŌåÆ
   447ŌåÆ	senders := defaultSenders()
   448ŌåÆ
   449ŌåÆ	msg := routing.Notification{
   450ŌåÆ		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("Semantic Release Report: %s %s", result.projectName, result.version),
   451ŌåÆ		Body:        result.reportText,
   452ŌåÆ		Version:     result.version,
   453ŌåÆ		ProjectName: result.projectName,
   454ŌåÆ	}
   455ŌåÆ
   456ŌåÆ	// Build ReleaseURL for the semantic release detail page.
   457ŌåÆ	if o.publicURL != "" {
   458ŌåÆ		msg.ReleaseURL = fmt.Sprintf("%s/projects/%s/semantic-releases/%s", o.publicURL, run.ProjectID, result.semanticReleaseID)
   459ŌåÆ	}
   460ŌåÆ
   461ŌåÆ	// Look up the first source for provider/repo info to build SourceURL.
   462ŌåÆ	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 1)
   463ŌåÆ	if err == nil && len(sources) > 0 {
   464ŌåÆ		msg.Provider = sources[0].Provider
   465ŌåÆ		msg.Repository = sources[0].Repository
   466ŌåÆ		msg.SourceURL = routing.ProviderURL(sources[0].Provider, sources[0].Repository, result.version)
   467ŌåÆ	}
   468ŌåÆ
   469ŌåÆ	for _, sub := range subs {
   470ŌåÆ		ch, err := o.store.GetChannel(ctx, sub.ChannelID)
   471ŌåÆ		if err != nil {
   472ŌåÆ			slog.Error("get channel for project notification",
   473ŌåÆ				"channel_id", sub.ChannelID, "err", err)
   474ŌåÆ			continue
   475ŌåÆ		}
   476ŌåÆ
   477ŌåÆ		sender, ok := senders[ch.Type]
   478ŌåÆ		if !ok {
   479ŌåÆ			slog.Warn("unknown channel type for project notification", "type", ch.Type)
   480ŌåÆ			continue
   481ŌåÆ		}
   482ŌåÆ
   483ŌåÆ		if err := sender.Send(ctx, ch, msg); err != nil {
   484ŌåÆ			slog.Error("send project notification failed",
   485ŌåÆ				"channel", ch.Name, "type", ch.Type, "err", err)
   486ŌåÆ		} else {
   487ŌåÆ			slog.Info("project notification sent",
   488ŌåÆ				"channel", ch.Name, "project_id", run.ProjectID, "version", result.version)
   489ŌåÆ		}
   490ŌåÆ	}
   491ŌåÆ}
   492ŌåÆ
   493ŌåÆ// parseReport attempts to parse the agent's text output as a SemanticReport JSON.
   494ŌåÆ// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   495ŌåÆfunc parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   496ŌåÆ	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   497ŌåÆ	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   498ŌåÆ	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   499ŌåÆ		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   500ŌåÆ		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   501ŌåÆ			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   502ŌåÆ		}
   503ŌåÆ		// Remove closing fence.
   504ŌåÆ		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   505ŌåÆ			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   506ŌåÆ		}
   507ŌåÆ		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   508ŌåÆ	}
   509ŌåÆ
   510ŌåÆ	var report models.SemanticReport
   511ŌåÆ	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   512ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   513ŌåÆ	}
   514ŌåÆ
   515ŌåÆ	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   516ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   517ŌåÆ	}
   518ŌåÆ
   519ŌåÆ	return &report, nil
   520ŌåÆ}
   521ŌåÆ

1ŌåÆpackage openai
     2ŌåÆ
     3ŌåÆimport (
     4ŌåÆ	"fmt"
     5ŌåÆ
     6ŌåÆ	"google.golang.org/genai"
     7ŌåÆ
     8ŌåÆ	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
     9ŌåÆ	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    10ŌåÆ)
    11ŌåÆ
    12ŌåÆ// WebSearch is a tool that enables OpenAI's built-in web search via the
    13ŌåÆ// Responses API. It mirrors geminitool.GoogleSearch by adding a GoogleSearch
    14ŌåÆ// marker to the request config, which the OpenAI model wrapper detects and
    15ŌåÆ// translates into a web_search tool entry. SearchContextSize and
    16ŌåÆ// AllowedDomains are read directly from this struct by the model wrapper.
    17ŌåÆtype WebSearch struct {
    18ŌåÆ	SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium", or "high"
    19ŌåÆ	AllowedDomains    []string // restrict search to these domains
    20ŌåÆ}
    21ŌåÆ
    22ŌåÆ// Name implements tool.Tool.
    23ŌåÆfunc (WebSearch) Name() string { return "web_search" }
    24ŌåÆ
    25ŌåÆ// Description implements tool.Tool.
    26ŌåÆfunc (WebSearch) Description() string {
    27ŌåÆ	return "Search the web for additional context about a release."
    28ŌåÆ}
    29ŌåÆ
    30ŌåÆ// IsLongRunning implements tool.Tool.
    31ŌåÆfunc (WebSearch) IsLongRunning() bool { return false }
    32ŌåÆ
    33ŌåÆ// ProcessRequest implements toolinternal.RequestProcessor by adding a
    34ŌåÆ// GoogleSearch marker to the LLM request. The OpenAI model wrapper
    35ŌåÆ// detects this marker and emits web_search_options in the HTTP body.
    36ŌåÆfunc (WebSearch) ProcessRequest(_ tool.Context, req *model.LLMRequest) error {
    37ŌåÆ	if req == nil {
    38ŌåÆ		return fmt.Errorf("llm request is nil")
    39ŌåÆ	}
    40ŌåÆ	if req.Config == nil {
    41ŌåÆ		req.Config = &genai.GenerateContentConfig{}
    42ŌåÆ	}
    43ŌåÆ	req.Config.Tools = append(req.Config.Tools, &genai.Tool{
    44ŌåÆ		GoogleSearch: &genai.GoogleSearch{},
    45ŌåÆ	})
    46ŌåÆ	return nil
    47ŌåÆ}
    48ŌåÆ

![Build](https://github.com/sentioxyz/releaseguard/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg) ![AI Co-Authored](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/ai-commits.json) ## What it does - **Discovers releases** by polling Docker Hub and GitHub on configurable intervals - **Routes notifications** to Slack, Discord, and webhooks the moment a new version lands - **Generates AI reports** via Google Gemini agents that research changelogs, assess risk, and summarize what changed - **Serves a dashboard** (Next.js) for managing projects, sources, subscriptions, and browsing releases in real time ## Architecture ```mermaid graph LR A["Ingestion
(polling)"] --> B["PostgreSQL +
River Queue"] B --> C["Agent
(ADK-Go)"] C --> D["Routing
(channels)"] A -.- A1["Docker Hub
GitHub"] B -.- B1["Transactional Outbox
LISTEN/NOTIFY ŌåÆ SSE"] C -.- C1["Gemini LLM
research tools"] D -.- D1["Slack ┬Ę Discord
Webhooks"] ``` Release insert and job enqueue happen in a single SQL transaction ŌĆö zero-loss guarantee. See [ARCH.md](ARCH.md), [API.md](API.md), and [DESIGN.md](DESIGN.md) for the full design. ## Quick start **Prerequisites:** Go 1.25+, Docker, Node.js 20+ ```bash # Start Postgres and the server (NO_AUTH mode) make dev # In another terminal ŌĆö start the frontend make frontend-install make frontend-dev ``` The API runs on `localhost:8080`, the dashboard on `localhost:3000`. ## Environment variables | Variable | Default | Purpose | |----------|---------|---------| | `DATABASE_URL` | `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable` | PostgreSQL connection | | `LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP server bind address | | `NO_AUTH` | _(unset)_ | Set to `true` to disable API key auth (development) | | `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Log level: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` | | `LLM_PROVIDER` | `gemini` | LLM provider: `gemini` or `openai` | | `LLM_MODEL` | `gemini-2.5-flash` / `gpt-5.2` | Model name (default depends on provider) | | `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Gemini API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=gemini`) | | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | OpenAI API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=openai`) | | `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | OpenAI-compatible API base URL | | `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` | `gpt-5-search-api` | Search-capable model for OpenAI web search sub-agent | ## Project structure ``` cmd/ server/ Entry point ŌĆö wires all layers together agent/ Agent CLI ŌĆö run agent analysis for a project internal/ agent/ ADK-Go agent orchestrator, tools, and worker openai/ OpenAI-compatible LLM provider api/ REST API, SSE, middleware, auth db/ Connection pool and migrations ingestion/ Polling sources (Docker Hub, GitHub Atom) models/ Shared domain types queue/ River job definitions and client routing/ Notification channels and delivery worker web/ Next.js dashboard (React + Tailwind + shadcn) scripts/ Integration test harness ``` ## Extending More providers (npm, PyPI, Helm, etc.) and channels (PagerDuty, email, etc.) are planned. Adding one is a single-interface implementation: **Add a registry provider** ŌĆö implement `IIngestionSource` in `internal/ingestion/source.go`: ```go type IIngestionSource interface { FetchNewReleases(ctx context.Context) ([]IngestionResult, error) } ``` **Add a notification channel** ŌĆö implement `Sender` in `internal/routing/sender.go`: ```go type Sender interface { Send(ctx context.Context, channel models.NotificationChannel, msg Message) error } ``` ## Useful commands ```bash make build # go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server make test # go test ./... make vet # go vet ./... make lint # alias for vet make integration-test # full integration test (spins up its own Postgres) make db-reset # drop and recreate the local database make frontend-build # build Next.js static export make agent-dev # run agent CLI for a specific project make clean # remove binary, stop containers, delete volumes ```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Design: Migrate OpenAI Adapter to Responses API

**Date:** 2026-03-04
**Status:** Approved

## Problem

The current OpenAI adapter uses the Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) with `web_search_options: {}` for web search. This has limitations:

- Requires a dedicated search-capable model (`gpt-5-search-api`) ŌĆö separate from the main model
- No control over search parameters (domain filtering, search context size, user location)
- Chat Completions search models are being superseded by the Responses API

## Solution

Migrate the OpenAI adapter from Chat Completions to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`), which:

- Supports `web_search` as a native tool alongside function tools
- Works with any supported model (gpt-5, gpt-4.1, o4-mini, etc.)
- Provides domain filtering (`allowed_domains`), `search_context_size`, and `user_location`
- Eliminates the need for a separate search model

## Scope

### Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` | Rewrite request/response types and `doRequest()` for Responses API |
| `internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go` | Update tests for new wire format |
| `internal/agent/openai/websearch.go` | Add configurable fields (SearchContextSize, AllowedDomains) |
| `internal/agent/model.go` | Remove `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig` |
| `internal/agent/orchestrator.go` | Simplify search sub-agent (same model, no separate model creation) |
| `cmd/server/main.go` | Remove `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var |

### Request Format (Before ŌåÆ After)

**Before (Chat Completions):**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_releases", ...}}],
  "web_search_options": {}
}
```

**After (Responses):**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "input": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [
    {"type": "function", "name": "get_releases", "parameters": {...}},
    {"type": "web_search", "search_context_size": "medium"}
  ]
}
```

### Response Format (Before ŌåÆ After)

**Before:** `choices[0].message.content` + `choices[0].message.tool_calls`

**After:** `output` array containing:
- `{type: "web_search_call", status: "completed"}` ŌĆö search invocation record
- `{type: "function_call", id, name, arguments}` ŌĆö function tool calls
- `{type: "message", content: [{type: "output_text", text, annotations}]}` ŌĆö final text with citations

### WebSearch Tool Enhancement

```go
type WebSearch struct {
    SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium" (default), "high"
    AllowedDomains    []string // optional domain filter
}
```

The `ProcessRequest` method will encode these fields into the tool config rather than using the GoogleSearch marker hack.

### Config Simplification

**Removed from LLMConfig:**
- `OpenAISearchModel` ŌĆö no longer needed; any model supports web search via tools

**Removed env vars:**
- `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` ŌĆö search model is the same as the main model

### Orchestrator Simplification

The search sub-agent no longer needs a separate model. In `BuildAgent()`:

```go
// Before: separate model for search
searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, LLMConfig{
    Provider: "openai", Model: "gpt-5-search-api", ...
})

// After: same model, web search is just a tool
searchAgent = llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
    Model: llmModel,  // same model as root/data agent
    Tools: []tool.Tool{oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}},
})
```

## Non-Goals

- Streaming support (not implemented today, stays that way)
- Deep research models (o3-deep-research etc.)
- Gemini adapter changes (unaffected)

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/verification-before-completion

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence ŌēĀ evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter ŌēĀ compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion ŌēĀ excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
Ō£ģ [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
ŌØī "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
Ō£ģ Write ŌåÆ Run (pass) ŌåÆ Revert fix ŌåÆ Run (MUST FAIL) ŌåÆ Restore ŌåÆ Run (pass)
ŌØī "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
Ō£ģ [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
ŌØī "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
Ō£ģ Re-read plan ŌåÆ Create checklist ŌåÆ Verify each ŌåÆ Report gaps or completion
ŌØī "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
Ō£ģ Agent reports success ŌåÆ Check VCS diff ŌåÆ Verify changes ŌåÆ Report actual state
ŌØī Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion ŌåÆ redirect ŌåÆ rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.
Running fresh verification now.

content.text:
All tests pass (exit 0), vet clean (exit 0). The OpenAI Responses API migration is fully implemented. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

**Migrated** the OpenAI adapter from Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`).

### Key commits:
- `ac97603` ŌĆö Core adapter rewrite: new types (`responsesRequest`, `inputItem`, `responseTool`, etc.), new conversion functions (`convertInputs`, `convertResponseTools`, `convertResponseOutput`), `doRequest` now POSTs to `/responses`
- `f539e72` ŌĆö Cleanup: removed dead `mapFinishReason`, fixed doc comment
- `17acf32` ŌĆö Removed `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig`, simplified `BuildAgent` to use same model for search
- `b53b05f` ŌĆö Removed stale `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` from README
- `dea3701` ŌĆö Marked design doc as implemented

### What changed:
| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) | Responses API (`/v1/responses`) |
| Separate search model (`gpt-5-search-api`) | Same model with `web_search` tool |
| `web_search_options: {}` (no control) | `{type: "web_search", search_context_size, filters}` |
| `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var | Removed ŌĆö not needed |
| `WebSearch{}` (no config) | `WebSearch{SearchContextSize, AllowedDomains}` |

content.text:
Found it. Line 75: `agentpkg.BuildAgent(ctx, store, project, llmConfig, "")` ŌĆö the version is hardcoded as empty string `""`. Then in `BuildAgent`, `strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)` replaces all `{{VERSION}}` placeholders with empty string, producing instructions like "analyzing version  of a software project" and "Focus ONLY on version ." ŌĆö which confuses the LLM.

This isn't a regression from our changes ŌĆö this was pre-existing. But let me check if there's a version flag that should exist.

content.content: No matches found

content.text:
There's no `--version` flag on the agent CLI. The dev CLI passes `""` as the version to `BuildAgent`, which causes the `{{VERSION}}` placeholders to become empty strings in the agent instruction. The LLM then sees "analyzing version  of a software project" and gets confused.

The fix is straightforward ŌĆö add a `--version` flag to the agent CLI.

	version := flag.String("version", "", "Target version to analyze (e.g. v1.10.15)")
	flag.Parse()

const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.

Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.

Use the available tools to:
1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
   (e.g., community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues).

CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting ŌĆö just the raw JSON.

For download_links: prefer direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) over generic release pages. Include the general release page URL as well, but prioritize direct download links when available.

The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
{
  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation for the SRE team",
  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15"],
  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
}`

content.text:
The instruction is version-centric ŌĆö every sentence references `{{VERSION}}`. When version is empty, it becomes nonsensical. The production path (`executeAgent`) also has this issue but handles it differently ŌĆö it falls back to "Analyze the recent releases" as the user prompt (line 325), but the system instruction still has empty placeholders.

The cleanest fix: when version is empty, use an alternative instruction that doesn't reference a specific version.
The fix should be in `BuildAgent` ŌĆö when version is empty, use a generic instruction instead of one riddled with empty `{{VERSION}}` placeholders.

Fixed. Two changes:

1. **`cmd/agent/main.go`** ŌĆö Added `--version` flag so you can now run:
   ```
   go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=<uuid> --version=v1.10.15 web
   ```

message.content: why the search_agent doesn't get invoked by default?

content.text: Let me investigate how the search agent is set up and why it might not be getting invoked.

The issue is in the search agent's description at line 132:

```
"Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources"
```

And the DefaultInstruction at line 55:

```
5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
```

Both explicitly tell the LLM to treat web search as a last resort. The root agent sees two tools ŌĆö `data_agent` and `search_agent` ŌĆö and the descriptions actively discourage search usage. The LLM reads "ONLY when" and almost never decides it's needed.

The fix is to make the instruction and description encourage search use rather than gatekeep it.

   - Fix 2: In `BuildAgent`, when version is empty, substitute `{{VERSION}}` with generic text instead of blank strings

9. **Current Question**: User asked "why the search_agent doesn't get invoked by default?"
   - I identified the issue: both the search agent description and DefaultInstruction contain discouraging language ("Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources")
   - I was about to propose a fix to the instruction/description wording when the summary was requested

Key files modified:
- `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` - Complete rewrite from Chat Completions to Responses API
- `internal/agent/openai/websearch.go` - Added SearchContextSize and AllowedDomains fields
- `internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go` - New tests for conversion functions
- `internal/agent/openai/websearch_test.go` - Updated test names
- `internal/agent/model.go` - Removed OpenAISearchModel, added wsConfig param
- `internal/agent/orchestrator.go` - Simplified search agent, fixed empty version handling

- `README.md` - Removed stale OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL reference
- `docs/plans/2026-03-04-openai-responses-api-design.md` - Design doc
- `docs/plans/2026-03-04-openai-responses-api.md` - Implementation plan

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user initially requested wrapping the OpenAI search tool into a standard tool or using a sub-agent, referencing `https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/tools-web-search/`. Through brainstorming, the intent was refined to: **migrate the OpenAI adapter from the Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`)** to gain native `web_search` tool support with domain filtering, search context size control, and elimination of the separate search model requirement. After implementation, the user reported two bugs: (1) the agent-dev CLI was broken due to empty version placeholders, and (2) the `search_agent` never gets invoked by default.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Google ADK-Go v0.5.0**: Agent framework implementing `model.LLM` interface with `GenerateContent(ctx, req, streaming) iter.Seq2[*model.LLMResponse, error]`
   - **Multi-agent orchestration**: Root agent (`release_analyst`) delegates to `data_agent` (DB queries) and `search_agent` (web search) via `agenttool.New()`
   - **OpenAI Responses API**: Uses `POST /v1/responses` with `input` items (not `messages`), flat tool definitions (not nested `function` key), `function_call`/`function_call_output` input items for tool results
   - **OpenAI Chat Completions API** (old): `POST /v1/chat/completions` with `messages`, `web_search_options: {}`, required special search models like `gpt-5-search-api`
   - **GoogleSearch marker pattern**: ADK tools use `ProcessRequest` to inject `genai.GoogleSearch{}` markers into `req.Config.Tools`, which the model wrapper detects and translates to provider-specific search config
   - **River job queue**: Agent runs are triggered via transactional outbox pattern with `AgentJobArgs`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`internal/agent/openai/openai.go`** ŌĆö Core OpenAI adapter implementing `model.LLM`. Completely rewritten from Chat Completions to Responses API.
     - Removed: `chatRequest`, `chatMessage`, `chatTool`, `chatFunction`, `chatResponse`, `chatChoice`, `chatUsage`, `webSearchOptions`, `toolCall`, `functionCall`, `convertContents`, `convertTools`, `convertResponse`, `hasWebSearch`, `mergeAssistantToolCalls`, `mapFinishReason`
     - Added: `responsesRequest`, `inputItem`, `responseTool`, `searchFilters`, `responsesResponse`, `outputItem`, `outputContent`, `annotation`, `respUsage`, `apiError`, `convertInputs`, `convertResponseTools`, `convertResponseOutput`
     - `NewModel` signature changed from 3 to 4 params: `NewModel(_ context.Context, modelName string, cfg Config, wsConfig *WebSearch) (model.LLM, error)`
     - `openaiModel` struct gained `wsConfig *WebSearch` field
     - `doRequest` now POSTs to `m.baseURL+"/responses"` instead of `/chat/completions`
     - Key conversion: ADK `FunctionCall` parts ŌåÆ `{type: "function_call", call_id, name, arguments}` input items; ADK `FunctionResponse` parts ŌåÆ `{type: "function_call_output", call_id, output}` input items
     ```go
     func convertInputs(req *model.LLMRequest) ([]inputItem, string) {
         var items []inputItem
         var instructions string
         if req.Config != nil && req.Config.SystemInstruction != nil {
             var text string
             for _, p := range req.Config.SystemInstruction.Parts {
                 if p.Text != "" { text += p.Text }
             }
             instructions = text
         }
         for _, c := range req.Contents {
             for _, p := range c.Parts {
                 switch {
                 case p.FunctionCall != nil:
                     argsJSON, _ := json.Marshal(p.FunctionCall.Args)
                     items = append(items, inputItem{Type: "function_call", CallID: p.FunctionCall.ID, Name: p.FunctionCall.Name, Arguments: string(argsJSON)})
                 case p.FunctionResponse != nil:
                     respJSON, _ := json.Marshal(p.FunctionResponse.Response)
                     items = append(items, inputItem{Type: "function_call_output", CallID: p.FunctionResponse.ID, Output: string(respJSON)})
                 case p.Text != "":
                     items = append(items, inputItem{Type: "message", Role: mapRole(c.Role), Content: p.Text})
                 }
             }
         }
         return items, instructions
     }
     ```

   - **`internal/agent/openai/websearch.go`** ŌĆö WebSearch tool definition. Added config fields.
     ```go
     type WebSearch struct {
         SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium", or "high"
         AllowedDomains    []string // restrict search to these domains
     }
     ```

   - **`internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go`** ŌĆö Tests for the adapter.
     - Removed: `TestConvertTools_SkipsGoogleSearchMarker`, `TestHasWebSearch`
     - Added: `TestConvertInputs_SystemInstruction`, `TestConvertInputs_FunctionCallAndResponse`, `TestConvertResponseTools_WebSearch`, `TestConvertResponseOutput_TextAndFunctionCalls`
     - Kept: `TestSchemaToMap_LowercasesType`

   - **`internal/agent/openai/websearch_test.go`** ŌĆö Tests for WebSearch tool. Renamed `TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest` ŌåÆ `TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest_SetsMarker`.

   - **`internal/agent/model.go`** ŌĆö LLM provider factory. Removed `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig`, added `wsConfig` parameter.
     ```go
     type LLMConfig struct {
         Provider     string
         Model        string
         GoogleAPIKey string
         OpenAIAPIKey  string
         OpenAIBaseURL string
     }
     func NewLLMModel(ctx context.Context, cfg LLMConfig, wsConfig *oaimodel.WebSearch) (model.LLM, error) {
     ```

   - **`internal/agent/orchestrator.go`** ŌĆö Agent lifecycle orchestration. Simplified `BuildAgent` to use same model for search, fixed empty version handling.
     ```go
     // Search sub-agent now uses same model with web search enabled
     case "openai":
         wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
         searchTool = *wsConfig
         searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
     ```
     ```go
     // Empty version handling
     if version != "" {
         instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
     } else {
         instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "version {{VERSION}} of", "the latest releases of")
         instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", "the latest version")
     }
     ```
     - The `DefaultInstruction` at line 55 says: `"5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources"`
     - The search agent description at line 132 says: `"Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources"`

   - **`cmd/agent/main.go`** ŌĆö Agent dev CLI. Added `--version` flag, passes `*version` to `BuildAgent`.

   - **`cmd/server/main.go`** ŌĆö Server entrypoint. Removed `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig` initialization.

   - **`README.md`** ŌĆö Removed stale `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var documentation.

   - **`docs/plans/2026-03-04-openai-responses-api-design.md`** ŌĆö Design document, status updated to "Implemented".

   - **`docs/plans/2026-03-04-openai-responses-api.md`** ŌĆö 9-task implementation plan.

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Dead `mapFinishReason` function**: Spec reviewer caught this leftover from Chat Completions. Removed it.
   - **Stale doc comment**: `GenerateContent` comment still said "chat completion request". Updated to "Responses API request".
   - **Stale README**: `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var still documented in README. Removed the line.
   - **Agent-dev broken (user-reported)**: `cmd/agent/main.go` passed empty string `""` as version to `BuildAgent`, causing `{{VERSION}}` placeholders to become blank. Fixed by adding `--version` flag and adding empty-version fallback logic in `BuildAgent`.
   - **Search agent not invoked (user-reported, current issue)**: The `search_agent` description and `DefaultInstruction` both say "ONLY when you need information not available from sources" ŌĆö this discouraging language causes the LLM to almost never invoke it. This is the current open issue.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Successfully migrated from Chat Completions to Responses API with full test coverage
   - Eliminated the separate search model requirement (`OpenAISearchModel` / `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL`)
   - Fixed the agent-dev CLI empty version placeholder bug
   - Currently investigating why `search_agent` is not invoked by the root agent

6. All User Messages:
   - "we should wrap the openai search tool into a standard tool or we should use sub-agent to handle it, please check https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/tools-web-search/ for reference"
   - Selected "Migrate to Responses API" (clarifying question answer)
   - Selected "Full Responses API (Recommended)" (approach selection)
   - Selected "Looks good, proceed" (design approval)
   - Selected "Subagent-Driven (Recommended)" (execution approach)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Fix the search agent not being invoked by default ŌĆö the root cause is the discouraging "ONLY when" language in both the `DefaultInstruction` (line 55) and the `search_agent` description (line 132) in `orchestrator.go`

8. Current Work:
   The user asked: **"why the search_agent doesn't get invoked by default?"**
   
   I identified the root cause: two places in `internal/agent/orchestrator.go` actively discourage the LLM from using the search agent:
   
   1. `DefaultInstruction` line 55: `"5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources"`
   2. Search agent description line 132: `"Search the web for additional context about a release. Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources, such as community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption statistics, or known issues."`

content.text:
The two culprits:

1. **Line 55**: `"5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources"`
2. **Line 132**: `"Search the web for additional context about a release. Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources, such as community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption statistics, or known issues."`

Both use restrictive "ONLY when" phrasing that tells the LLM to treat search as a last resort. The fix is to make search a default step in the analysis workflow, not a conditional one.

content.text: Now let me verify the build still passes.

Build passes. Here's what was changed and why:

**Root cause:** The `DefaultInstruction` and search agent `Description` both used "ONLY when" language, which tells the LLM to treat web search as a last resort. Since the root agent decides whether to delegate to sub-agents based on their descriptions and the system instruction, this phrasing caused it to almost never invoke `search_agent`.

**Fix (two lines in `orchestrator.go`):**

1. **Instruction (line 55):** Changed from _"Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources"_ ŌåÆ _"Always search the web for supplementary context about this release"_ ŌĆö makes search a default step, not a conditional one.

2. **Search agent description (line 132):** Changed from _"Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources"_ ŌåÆ _"Use this for every release analysis to enrich the report with external insights"_ ŌĆö tells the root agent to always delegate to search.

content.text:
Build passes. The fix explicitly tells the LLM what `download_links` should and should not contain:

- **Should:** direct binary/artifact download URLs, GitHub/project release page URLs
- **Should not:** CVE links, security advisory URLs (nvd.nist.gov, osv.dev), blog posts, documentation

The previous instruction only said "prefer" direct downloads but never excluded non-download URLs, so the LLM treated any URL it found as fair game for that field.

content.text:
The search agent doesn't know what project it's analyzing ŌĆö it's searching the web for "v3.0.8" without any project context, so it gets ambiguous results across unrelated projects. The search agent needs the project name in its instruction.

Let me check how the project name is available.

The search agent has no `Instruction` set, so it has no idea which project it's working on. When the root agent delegates "search for v3.0.8", the search agent just blindly searches the web for "v3.0.8" with no project context. The fix is to give the search agent an instruction that includes the project name.

    45ŌåÆ
    46ŌåÆconst DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.
    47ŌåÆ
    48ŌåÆFocus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.
    49ŌåÆ
    50ŌåÆUse the available tools to:
    51ŌåÆ1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
    52ŌåÆ2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
    53ŌåÆ3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
    54ŌåÆ4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
    55ŌåÆ5. Always search the web for supplementary context about this release
    56ŌåÆ   (community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues, migration guides).
    57ŌåÆ
    58ŌåÆCRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
    59ŌåÆDo not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting ŌĆö just the raw JSON.
    60ŌåÆ
    61ŌåÆFor download_links: ONLY include URLs where the user can actually download the software ŌĆö direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) and the GitHub/project release page. Do NOT include CVE links, security advisory URLs, blog posts, documentation, or any non-download URLs.
    62ŌåÆ
    63ŌåÆThe JSON object must have exactly these fields:

    71ŌåÆ  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",

    73ŌåÆ  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15"],

    75ŌåÆ}`
    76ŌåÆ
    77ŌåÆ// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    78ŌåÆ// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    79ŌåÆ// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    80ŌåÆfunc BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    81ŌåÆ	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    82ŌåÆ	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    83ŌåÆ		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    84ŌåÆ	}
    85ŌåÆ	if version != "" {
    86ŌåÆ		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    87ŌåÆ	} else {
    88ŌåÆ		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "version {{VERSION}} of", "the latest releases of")
    89ŌåÆ		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", "the latest version")
    90ŌåÆ	}
    91ŌåÆ
    92ŌåÆ	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, nil)
    93ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
    94ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    95ŌåÆ	}
    96ŌåÆ
    97ŌåÆ	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    98ŌåÆ	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    99ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   100ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
   101ŌåÆ	}
   102ŌåÆ
   103ŌåÆ	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
   104ŌåÆ	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   105ŌåÆ		Name:        "data_agent",
   106ŌåÆ		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   107ŌåÆ		Model:       llmModel,
   108ŌåÆ		Tools:       functionTools,
   109ŌåÆ	})
   110ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   111ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   112ŌåÆ	}
   113ŌåÆ
   114ŌåÆ	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   115ŌåÆ	var searchTool tool.Tool
   116ŌåÆ	var searchModel model.LLM
   117ŌåÆ	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   118ŌåÆ	case "openai":
   119ŌåÆ		wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
   120ŌåÆ		searchTool = *wsConfig
   121ŌåÆ		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
   122ŌåÆ		if err != nil {
   123ŌåÆ			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   124ŌåÆ		}
   125ŌåÆ	default: // gemini
   126ŌåÆ		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   127ŌåÆ		searchModel = llmModel
   128ŌåÆ	}
   129ŌåÆ
   130ŌåÆ	searchInstruction := fmt.Sprintf(
   131ŌåÆ		"You are a web search assistant for the project %q. Always include the project name in your search queries to get relevant results. Focus on this specific project ŌĆö ignore results for unrelated projects that happen to share the same version number.",
   132ŌåÆ		project.Name,
   133ŌåÆ	)
   134ŌåÆ
   135ŌåÆ	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   136ŌåÆ		Name:        "search_agent",
   137ŌåÆ		Description: "Search the web for context about a release ŌĆö community sentiment, security advisories, adoption statistics, known issues, and migration guides. Use this for every release analysis to enrich the report with external insights.",
   138ŌåÆ		Instruction: searchInstruction,
   139ŌåÆ		Model:       searchModel,
   140ŌåÆ		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   141ŌåÆ	})
   142ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   143ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   144ŌåÆ	}
   145ŌåÆ
   146ŌåÆ	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   147ŌåÆ	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   148ŌåÆ		Name:        "release_analyst",
   149ŌåÆ		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   150ŌåÆ		Model:       llmModel,
   151ŌåÆ		Instruction: instruction,
   152ŌåÆ		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   153ŌåÆ			agenttool.New(dataAgent, nil),
   154ŌåÆ			agenttool.New(searchAgent, nil),
   155ŌåÆ		},
   156ŌåÆ	})
   157ŌåÆ}

   159ŌåÆ// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   160ŌåÆ// release matching the target version.
   161ŌåÆfunc (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   162ŌåÆ	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   163ŌåÆ	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   164ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   165ŌåÆ		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   166ŌåÆ	}
   167ŌåÆ	if len(sources) == 0 {
   168ŌåÆ		return true, nil
   169ŌåÆ	}
   170ŌåÆ
   171ŌåÆ	for _, src := range sources {
   172ŌåÆ		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   173ŌåÆ		if err != nil {
   174ŌåÆ			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   175ŌåÆ		}
   176ŌåÆ		if !has {
   177ŌåÆ			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",

   180ŌåÆ				"version", version,
   181ŌåÆ			)

   183ŌåÆ		}

   187ŌåÆ
   188ŌåÆ// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   189ŌåÆ// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   190ŌåÆ// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   191ŌåÆtype Orchestrator struct {
   192ŌåÆ	store     OrchestratorStore
   193ŌåÆ	llmConfig LLMConfig
   194ŌåÆ	publicURL string // base URL for internal Changelogue links (e.g. "https://changelogue.example.com")
   195ŌåÆ}
   196ŌåÆ
   197ŌåÆ// NewOrchestrator creates a new Orchestrator. It validates that the provided
   198ŌåÆ// LLMConfig has the required API key for the configured provider. If the key
   199ŌåÆ// is missing, it returns an error so the caller can degrade gracefully.
   200ŌåÆfunc NewOrchestrator(store OrchestratorStore, cfg LLMConfig, publicURL string) (*Orchestrator, error) {
   201ŌåÆ	switch cfg.Provider {
   202ŌåÆ	case "openai":
   203ŌåÆ		if cfg.OpenAIAPIKey == "" {
   204ŌåÆ			return nil, fmt.Errorf("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   205ŌåÆ		}
   206ŌåÆ	default: // gemini
   207ŌåÆ		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
   208ŌåÆ			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   209ŌåÆ		}
   210ŌåÆ	}
   211ŌåÆ	return &Orchestrator{
   212ŌåÆ		store:     store,
   213ŌåÆ		llmConfig: cfg,
   214ŌåÆ		publicURL: strings.TrimRight(publicURL, "/"),
   215ŌåÆ	}, nil
   216ŌåÆ}
   217ŌåÆ
   218ŌåÆ// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   219ŌåÆ//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   220ŌåÆ//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   221ŌåÆ//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   222ŌåÆ//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   223ŌåÆ//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   224ŌåÆ//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   225ŌåÆ//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   226ŌåÆfunc (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   227ŌåÆ	// Mark as running.
   228ŌåÆ	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   229ŌåÆ		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   230ŌåÆ	}
   231ŌåÆ
   232ŌåÆ	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   233ŌåÆ	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   234ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   235ŌåÆ		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   236ŌåÆ		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   237ŌåÆ		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   238ŌåÆ			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   239ŌåÆ				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   240ŌåÆ		}
   241ŌåÆ		return err
   242ŌåÆ	}
   243ŌåÆ
   244ŌåÆ	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   245ŌåÆ	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   246ŌåÆ		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   247ŌåÆ	}
   248ŌåÆ	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   249ŌåÆ		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   250ŌåÆ	}
   251ŌåÆ
   252ŌåÆ	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   253ŌåÆ	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   254ŌåÆ
   255ŌåÆ	return nil
   256ŌåÆ}
   257ŌåÆ
   258ŌåÆ// agentResult holds the output of a successful agent execution, used to
   259ŌåÆ// construct notifications after the semantic release is persisted.
   260ŌåÆtype agentResult struct {
   261ŌåÆ	semanticReleaseID string
   262ŌåÆ	version           string
   263ŌåÆ	reportText        string
   264ŌåÆ	projectName       string

   266ŌåÆ
   267ŌåÆ// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   268ŌåÆ// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   269ŌåÆfunc (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   270ŌåÆ	// Load project.
   271ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   272ŌåÆ	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   273ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   274ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   275ŌåÆ	}
   276ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   277ŌåÆ
   278ŌåÆ	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   279ŌåÆ	version := run.Version
   280ŌåÆ	if version == "" {
   281ŌåÆ		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   282ŌåÆ		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   283ŌåÆ			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   284ŌåÆ		}
   285ŌåÆ	}
   286ŌåÆ
   287ŌåÆ	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   288ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   289ŌåÆ		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   290ŌåÆ		"version", version)
   291ŌåÆ	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   292ŌåÆ	if err != nil {

   294ŌåÆ	}

   297ŌåÆ	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   298ŌåÆ	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   299ŌåÆ		AppName: "changelogue",
   300ŌåÆ		UserID:  "system",
   301ŌåÆ	})
   302ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   303ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   304ŌåÆ	}
   305ŌåÆ	sess := createResp.Session
   306ŌåÆ
   307ŌåÆ	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   308ŌåÆ	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   309ŌåÆ		AppName:        "changelogue",
   310ŌåÆ		Agent:          agentInstance,
   311ŌåÆ		SessionService: sessionService,
   312ŌåÆ		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   313ŌåÆ			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   314ŌåÆ			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   315ŌåÆ		},
   316ŌåÆ	})
   317ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   318ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   319ŌåÆ	}

   341ŌåÆ		}
   342ŌåÆ		eventCount++
   343ŌåÆ		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   344ŌåÆ			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   345ŌåÆ				if part.Text != "" {

   347ŌåÆ				}
   348ŌåÆ			}
   349ŌåÆ		}
   350ŌåÆ	}
   351ŌåÆ	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",

   353ŌåÆ		"event_count", eventCount,

   431ŌåÆ// defaultSenders returns a map of channel type to Sender for the supported
   432ŌåÆ// notification channel types (webhook, slack, discord).
   433ŌåÆfunc defaultSenders() map[string]routing.Sender {
   434ŌåÆ	httpClient := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   435ŌåÆ	return map[string]routing.Sender{
   436ŌåÆ		"webhook": &routing.WebhookSender{Client: httpClient},
   437ŌåÆ		"slack":   &routing.SlackSender{Client: httpClient},

   439ŌåÆ	}
   440ŌåÆ}
   441ŌåÆ
   442ŌåÆ// sendProjectNotifications looks up project-level subscriptions and sends a
   443ŌåÆ// notification to each channel with the semantic release report. This is
   444ŌåÆ// best-effort: errors are logged but do not propagate to the caller.
   445ŌåÆfunc (o *Orchestrator) sendProjectNotifications(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun, result *agentResult) {
   446ŌåÆ	subs, err := o.store.ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   447ŌåÆ	if err != nil {
   448ŌåÆ		slog.Error("list project subscriptions for notification",
   449ŌåÆ			"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
   450ŌåÆ		return
   451ŌåÆ	}
   452ŌåÆ	if len(subs) == 0 {
   453ŌåÆ		slog.Debug("agent: no project subscriptions configured",
   454ŌåÆ			"project_id", run.ProjectID)
   455ŌåÆ		return
   456ŌåÆ	}
   457ŌåÆ
   458ŌåÆ	senders := defaultSenders()
   459ŌåÆ
   460ŌåÆ	msg := routing.Notification{
   461ŌåÆ		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("Semantic Release Report: %s %s", result.projectName, result.version),
   462ŌåÆ		Body:        result.reportText,
   463ŌåÆ		Version:     result.version,
   464ŌåÆ		ProjectName: result.projectName,
   465ŌåÆ	}
   466ŌåÆ
   467ŌåÆ	// Build ReleaseURL for the semantic release detail page.
   468ŌåÆ	if o.publicURL != "" {
   469ŌåÆ		msg.ReleaseURL = fmt.Sprintf("%s/projects/%s/semantic-releases/%s", o.publicURL, run.ProjectID, result.semanticReleaseID)
   470ŌåÆ	}
   471ŌåÆ
   472ŌåÆ	// Look up the first source for provider/repo info to build SourceURL.
   473ŌåÆ	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 1)
   474ŌåÆ	if err == nil && len(sources) > 0 {
   475ŌåÆ		msg.Provider = sources[0].Provider
   476ŌåÆ		msg.Repository = sources[0].Repository
   477ŌåÆ		msg.SourceURL = routing.ProviderURL(sources[0].Provider, sources[0].Repository, result.version)
   478ŌåÆ	}
   479ŌåÆ
   480ŌåÆ	for _, sub := range subs {
   481ŌåÆ		ch, err := o.store.GetChannel(ctx, sub.ChannelID)
   482ŌåÆ		if err != nil {
   483ŌåÆ			slog.Error("get channel for project notification",
   484ŌåÆ				"channel_id", sub.ChannelID, "err", err)
   485ŌåÆ			continue
   486ŌåÆ		}
   487ŌåÆ
   488ŌåÆ		sender, ok := senders[ch.Type]
   489ŌåÆ		if !ok {
   490ŌåÆ			slog.Warn("unknown channel type for project notification", "type", ch.Type)
   491ŌåÆ			continue
   492ŌåÆ		}
   493ŌåÆ
   494ŌåÆ		if err := sender.Send(ctx, ch, msg); err != nil {
   495ŌåÆ			slog.Error("send project notification failed",
   496ŌåÆ				"channel", ch.Name, "type", ch.Type, "err", err)
   497ŌåÆ		} else {

   500ŌåÆ		}
   501ŌåÆ	}
   502ŌåÆ}

   504ŌåÆ// parseReport attempts to parse the agent's text output as a SemanticReport JSON.
   505ŌåÆ// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   506ŌåÆfunc parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   507ŌåÆ	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   508ŌåÆ	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   509ŌåÆ	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   510ŌåÆ		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   511ŌåÆ		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   512ŌåÆ			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   513ŌåÆ		}
   514ŌåÆ		// Remove closing fence.
   515ŌåÆ		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   516ŌåÆ			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   517ŌåÆ		}
   518ŌåÆ		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   519ŌåÆ	}
   520ŌåÆ
   521ŌåÆ	var report models.SemanticReport
   522ŌåÆ	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   523ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   524ŌåÆ	}
   525ŌåÆ
   526ŌåÆ	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   527ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   528ŌåÆ	}
   529ŌåÆ
   530ŌåÆ	return &report, nil
   531ŌåÆ}
